concerns.text: FR-001 and FR-006 mandate ingesting 'actual' VR logs and merging real datasets (MFQ/Moral Stories). T014/T015 implement 'Validation-Only' simulation, and T019b defaults execution to simulation. This silently weakens the requirement to ingest real data, violating the spec's core mandate for empirical data ingestion in the current phase.

T019a defines architecture for real data but T019b explicitly defaults to simulation. The spec (FR-001/FR-006) requires the system to ingest real data. Deferring the actual ingestion to a 'future phase' without an explicit 'staged' marker in the spec constitutes a silent relaxation of the functional requirement.

FR-001 and US-1 require logging exclusion reasons and VR mapping logs. T009 ('Implement base logging infrastructure') is marked complete but lacks a specific deliverable artifact (e.g., `code/utils/logging.py` or config) to verify the mechanism exists, risking a failure to meet the data hygiene and traceability requirements of the spec.

The plan and spec require the pipeline to complete within 6 hours on a CPU-only runner. T037 (performance profiling) is marked incomplete. Without this task, the performance constraint (a hard requirement for the CI environment) is unverified and potentially unmet.

Constitution Principle V (Versioning Discipline) requires all artifacts to be checksummed. T040 (validation of checksums) is incomplete. This leaves the 'Single Source of Truth' constraint unverified, as there is no task ensuring the checksums are actually valid and recorded.
